title: garrya laurifolia subsp. racemosa
intro: Garrya laurifolia subsp. racemosa, also known as the coastal redbush or silk tassel bush, is a plant species native to the Pacific coast of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant. Garrya laurifolia subsp. racemosa can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used by indigenous peoples for its medicinal and cultural significance.
